discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape driven by a sharp semiconductor rally (SMH +5%) with VIX collapsing 12%. Megacaps mostly red while chips and Tesla lead; defensives (utilities, REITs, materials) under pressure. The mid-cap movers are all semis riding the sector wave, with no company-specific catalysts visible.

notable_index_moves: SMH +5% — major chip rally, likely Micron-led memory trade re-rating
VIX -12% to 18.9, signaling broad de-risking unwind
XLU -1.87%, XLRE -1.5%, XLB -1.32% — defensive/rate-sensitive rotation out
QQQ +1.56% but megacap tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META) all red — narrow strength concentrated in semis

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semiconductor equipment name riding sector-wide chip rally
discoveries.thesis: Move is consistent with SMH +5% and the broader memory/semicap re-rating sparked by Micron commentary on long-term supply agreements. ONTO is leveraged to memory capex (HBM inspection), so it's a logical beneficiary. No company-specific catalyst — magnitude is roughly in line with sector beta given semicap names typically run ~1.2-1.5x SMH on up days.
discoveries.what_confirms: Follow-through tomorrow if memory peers (LRCX, KLAC, AMAT) extend; sell-side notes specifically calling out HBM inspection capacity tightness
discoveries.what_kills: If SMH gives back gains tomorrow and ONTO holds — would suggest a company-specific bid we missed (M&A, contract win)
discoveries.catalyst: Sector sympathy with Micron-led memory rally; no 8-K, no scheduled earnings
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: MarketWatch piece on Micron and long-term memory supply agreements aligns timing-wise with SMH +5% and ONTO's HBM-leverage profile
research_pointers: Check ONTO's HBM/advanced packaging revenue mix in latest 10-Q
Compare today's move vs LRCX/KLAC/AMAT to gauge whether it's pure beta or premium
Look for any sell-side price target revisions tied to memory capex

discoveries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
discoveries.setup: Beaten-down semi catching up to sector on broad chip bid
discoveries.thesis: LSCC was down 5-day before today and is closing the gap with the SMH rally. Move is consistent with short-covering on a low-volatility risk-on day rather than a fundamental catalyst — no 8-K, no earnings scheduled. The fact it underperformed the 5-day window while peers rallied suggests today is mean-reversion within the sector.
discoveries.what_confirms: If LSCC continues to outperform SMH over next 2-3 sessions (genuine catch-up)
discoveries.what_kills: If it fades intraday tomorrow back to flat vs SMH — confirms one-day technical bounce
discoveries.catalyst: Semi sector sympathy/catch-up trade; no company-specific signal
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + 5-day return materially lagged sector = classic beta catch-up on a strong tape day
research_pointers: Check FPGA end-market commentary from Lattice peers (Altera/Intel)
Look for any short interest data — would explain bounce mechanics

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Micron memory rally and 'memory trade is alive' analyst narrative driving SMH +5%

discovery.no_signals_note: All four movers are semis with empty catalyst_signals, riding a sector-wide chip rally tied to Micron/memory narrative. No mid-cap mispricings with conviction tonight — the moves are roughly proportional to sector beta. Flagging as RATIONAL rather than manufacturing directional calls.

ai_announcements.headline: Apple WWDC 2026: Siri AI overhaul, Apple Intelligence expansion, AI Shortcuts, free cloud API for small devs
ai_announcements.summary: Apple announced a major Siri AI revamp, dedicated Siri app, AI-powered Shortcuts workflow builder, improved Image Playground, AI photo editing (Reframe), AI in Safari/Passwords, Siri-in-Camera bill splitting, and a waiver of cloud API costs for small developers (<2M downloads).

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Onto makes semiconductor metrology/inspection equipment. Apple's AI push (on-device + cloud) increases demand for advanced-node silicon, which benefits semicap broadly. But the link to today's WWDC news is indirect — move is more likely sector/semicap rotation than a direct WWDC reaction.
research_pointers: Check if SOX/SMH moved similarly today
Look for any Apple supply chain capex commentary
No specific WWDC item names Onto-relevant fabs

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Lattice FPGAs serve edge/industrial/comms — minimal direct exposure to Apple's consumer AI. Move likely sympathy with semis rally, not WWDC-specific.
research_pointers: Lattice 5-day is -1.27%, so today is a bounce, not WWDC-driven momentum

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco's MOCVD/laser annealing tied to advanced packaging/HBM — benefits from AI capex generally, not WWDC specifically. Volume is below average (0.7x), suggesting drift not catalyst.
research_pointers: Sub-average volume argues against news-driven move

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Extreme Networks is enterprise networking — no obvious WWDC linkage. Move is unrelated to today's AI news.
research_pointers: Look for company-specific news, analyst upgrade, or networking peer sympathy (ANET, CSCO)

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: WWDC 2026 was the dominant AI event but most announcements are consumer-facing feature updates with no clear public-stock loser. None of the four moving stocks listed have direct WWDC linkage — moves appear to be broad semis/tech rotation rather than reactions to specific AI announcements. No clear OVERDONE selloff or UNDERDONE rally candidates in today's tape. The Apple free-cloud-API-for-small-devs item is mildly negative for AWS/Azure/GCP at the margin but far too small to matter. No Anthropic news today.
